request.question: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?

title: Rain-X
Avery Berkel
ITW David Speer Academy
Shannon Free Zone
David Speer
W. James Farrell
Provena St. Mary's Hospital
Atossa Genetics
Hologic
Illinois Tool Works
sentences: Rain-X is a brand of consumer automotive and surface care products, including wiper blades owned by ITW Global Brands.
Until 2011 it was owned by Pennzoil-Quaker State Company dba SOPUS Products, a subsidiary of Royal Dutch Shell.
On March 1, 2011, Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has acquired SOPUS Products' Car Care business.
It became ITW Global, a division of Illinois Tool Works.

Avery Berkel, a major manufacturer of weighing systems and food processing equipment, was formed in 1993 when the British conglomerate General Electric Company plc combined their GEC Avery Ltd. (formerly W & T Avery Ltd.) business with the newly acquired Berkel company.
The group continued as a subsidiary of GEC (and later Marconi plc) until March 2000 when it was sold to Weigh-Tronix for £102.5 million to create Avery Weigh-Tronix.
In 2007, Avery Weigh-Tronix sold Avery Berkel to Illinois Tool Works.
ITW David Speer Academy (colloquially, Speer Academy) is a public four-year charter high school in the Belmont Cragin neighborhood in Chicago, Illinois.
It is a part of the Noble Network of Charter Schools.
It is named in memory of David B. Speer, the former Chairman of Illinois Tool Works (ITW), who served the company for 33 years until his death in 2012.
The school opened in 2014.
Shannon Free Zone is a 2.43 km2 , international business park adjacent to Shannon Airport, County Clare, Ireland which is 18 km from Ennis and 20 km from Limerick.
Businesses based on the site enjoyed special tax incentives on staff and profits until 2003.
This attracted a large number of multinational companies.
Currently there are over 100 international firms and 6,500 people employed at Shannon Free Zone in a diverse range of activities.

Companies who have invested at Shannon include Avocent, DeBeers Industrial Diamonds (Now Element Six), Kraus & Naimer, GE Capital, Precision Castparts Corp., Genworth, Ingersoll Rand, Intel, John Crane, Lufthansa Technik, Mentor Graphics, Molex, Illinois Tool Works, RSA Security, Ohshima, Schwarz Pharma and Zimmer.
David Blakeney Speer (April 6, 1951November 17, 2012) was an American businessman, best known as the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 2005-2012.
W. James Farrell (born April 1942) is an American businessman, known for being the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 1995 to 2005.
Provena St. Mary's Hospital is a non-profit hospital in Kankakee, Illinois.
It is a 182-bed acute care facility with a Level II Emergency/Trauma Center.

The hospital provides many health care services such as a Regional Cancer Center, Rehabilitation Services, Family Birthing Center, Pediatric Unit, Mental Health Services, Sleep Disorder Center, Breast Health Center, Renal Dialysis Centre, Accredited Chest Pain Center, Wound Care, Infusion Center, Breast Health Center, Occupational Health, Spirit of Women Program, and a Total Wellness Center.
It is part of the Presence Health healthcare system.
Provena St. Mary's is accredited by the Joint Commission and the Society of Chest Pain Centers (SCPC), as well as the American College of Surgeons Commission on Cancer.
Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.
The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.
The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.

Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.

While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".
Illinois Tool Works Inc. or ITW is a Fortune 500 company that produces engineered fasteners and components, equipment and consumable systems, and specialty products.
It was founded in 1912 by Byron L. Smith.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?
hop1_docs: Hologic | Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.  The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.  Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.  While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".

International Journal of Women's Health | The International Journal of Women's Health is a peer-reviewed healthcare journal focusing on all aspects of women's health care, including obstetrics, gynecology, and breast cancer.  The editor-in-chief is Elie D. Al-Chaer (University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences).  The journal is published by Dove Medical Press and it is abstracted and indexed in EMBASE and Scopus.

Women's medicine in antiquity | Childbirth and obstetrics in Classical Antiquity (here meaning the ancient Greco-Roman world) were studied by the physicians of ancient Greece and Rome.  Their ideas and practices during this time endured in Western medicine for centuries and many themes are seen in modern women's health.  Gynecology and obstetrics were originally studied and taught mainly by midwives in the ancient world, but eventually scholarly physicians of both sexes became involved as well.  Obstetrics is traditionally defined as the surgical specialty dealing with the care of a woman and her offspring during pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium (recovery).  Gynecology involves the medical practices dealing with the health of women's reproductive organs (vagina, uterus, ovaries) and their breasts.

Patient Health Questionnaire | The Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ) is a multiple-choice self-report inventory copyrighted by Pfizer Inc, that is used as a screening and diagnostic tool for mental health disorders of depression, anxiety, alcohol, eating, and somatoform disorders.  It is the self-report version of the Primary Care Evaluation of Mental Disorders (PRIME-MD), a diagnostic tool developed in the mid-1990s by Pfizer Inc.  The length of the original assessment limited its feasibility; consequently, a shorter version, consisting of 11 multi-part questions - the Patient Health Questionnaire was developed and validated.

Epocrates | Epocrates, Inc. is an American company based in San Francisco, California that makes various mobile health software applications under the "epocrates" name, mostly for health care providers, providing them reference information about drugs and diseases, and diagnostic tools.

Diagnostic Health Corporation | Diagnostic Health Corporation, based in Birmingham, Alabama, is one of the nation's largest independent diagnostic imaging companies.  The company is the former diagnostic division of HealthSouth Corporation.  The company has network of 53 free standing diagnostic imaging centers located in 19 states and the District of Columbia.

Atossa Genetics | Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.  The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
response.summary_1:
Primary topic: Hologic — corporate focus on diagnostics and women's/skeletal health.

- Hologic: "Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products."  
- Hologic: "core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health."  
- Hologic major products listed: "Selenia Dimension mammography system"; "Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers"; "NovaSure endometrial ablation device"; "ThinPrep pap test"; "Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays."  
- Ambiguity / missing fact: "Illinois Tool Works" is not mentioned in the provided passages — missing the bridge fact "Illinois Tool Works' business focus or segments."  
- Next-hop retrievals (priority order):  
  1. Fetch "Hologic" — Wikipedia lede/infobox and "Products" or "Core business units"/"History" sections to confirm wording.  
  2. Fetch "Illinois Tool Works" — Wikipedia lede/infobox and "Business segments" or "Products" sections to determine its corporate focus.  
  3. Search: "Illinois Tool Works business focus diagnostics breast health gynecology skeletal" (if Wikipedia page incomplete).
hop2_docs: Rain-X | Rain-X is a brand of consumer automotive and surface care products, including wiper blades owned by ITW Global Brands.  Until 2011 it was owned by Pennzoil-Quaker State Company dba SOPUS Products, a subsidiary of Royal Dutch Shell.  On March 1, 2011, Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has acquired SOPUS Products' Car Care business.  It became ITW Global, a division of Illinois Tool Works.

Illinois Tool Works | Illinois Tool Works Inc. or ITW is a Fortune 500 company that produces engineered fasteners and components, equipment and consumable systems, and specialty products.  It was founded in 1912 by Byron L. Smith.

Diamond segment | Diamond segments are the function parts of a metal-bonded diamond tool.  The metal-bonded diamond tool can be a metal-bonded diamond blade, a diamond grinding cup wheel, a diamond core drill bit, a diamond gang saw blade, etc.  The diamonds of a metal-bonded diamond tool are all in the tool's diamond segments to play their role of cutting or grinding.

The Value Model | The Value Model™ (VM) is a holistic model of how to create unrivalled customer value in products, systems and services.  The Value Model explains how customer value works and how customer value is made a practical tool – from market segmentation and discovering the business opportunity all the way to product launch.  The model as a whole handles agile product development in a multi project environment.  It is sometimes described as the core of UX design.

Aviation Safety Knowledge Management Environment | The Aviation Safety Knowledge Management Environment (ASKME) is a suite of information technology tools designed to support and enable the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) Aircraft Certification Service (AIR) to more efficiently certify new aircraft and modifications to existing aircraft.  The program was established to provide a comprehensive automation environment for critical safety business processes for the Office of Aviation Safety through deployment of 18 integrated business solutions (18 projects) between fiscal year 2008 and fiscal year 2017.  Segment 1 covers fiscal years FY08-FY12 and Segment 2 covers fiscal years FY13-FY17.  ASKME, Segment 2, obtained its baseline decision on September 21, 2011 from the FAA Joint Resources Council.

Illinois Hispanic Chamber of Commerce | Established in 1990, The Illinois Hispanic Chamber of Commerce (IHCC) has grown to become one of the largest and most influential business organizations and is by far the leading Hispanic business group in the state of Illinois.  IHCC works to generate resources and advocate on behalf of Hispanic businesses and is the preferred source of guidance, assistance, and networking opportunities for Hispanic business owners in Illinois.

Catamaran Corporation | Catamaran Corporation (formerly SXC Health Solutions) is a Schaumburg, Illinois based pharmacy benefit management company that sells services related to pharmacy benefit management and medical record keeping to businesses in the United States.  Working independently of the government and insurance companies allows it to operate as a third party verifier; the RxCLAIM online claim processing system allows for prescription drug claims to be processed online if the customer lives in and fills his/her prescription in the United States.  SXC has three separate but interrelated business segments which deal with prescription drug programs.  For 2013 23% of company revenue came from Cigna Corporation.
response.summary_2: Primary topic: Hologic — corporate focus on diagnostics and women's/skeletal health. Illinois Tool Works is a Fortune 500 company that produces engineered fasteners and components, equipment and consumable systems, and specialty products.
